# Test Strategy Guide

## Test Pyramid

```
          /\
         /e2e\           (NOT generated by test-kit — manual or Playwright)
        /------\
       /  int.  \        (Integration tests: API endpoint tests, DB tests)
      /----------\
     /    unit    \      (Unit tests: service logic, business rules)
    /--------------\
```

Generate: Unit + Integration.
E2E tests are out of scope for test-kit automation.

## Test Naming Convention

```
<MethodName>_when<Condition>_should<ExpectedBehavior>
```

Examples:
- `createOrder_whenStockAvailable_shouldReturnOrderWithPendingStatus`
- `cancelOrder_whenOrderAlreadyShipped_shouldThrowIllegalStateException`
- `GET_orders_whenUnauthenticated_shouldReturn401`

## Coverage Targets

| Layer | Target |
|-------|--------|
| Service (unit) | Every `public` method, every business rule |
| Controller (integration) | Every endpoint: happy + at least 1 error case |
| Repository | Custom JPQL/SQL queries only |
| Frontend | Components with logic (forms, data tables) |

## Story → Test Mapping

For each story (US-NNN):
1. **1 unit test per acceptance criterion** (happy path)
2. **1 unit test per business rule referenced** (rule violation)
3. **1 integration test for main API endpoint** (POST/GET/etc.)
4. **2 edge case tests** (boundary values, null inputs, concurrent access)

Minimum per story: 3 + number of ACs tests

## Test Data Strategy

- Use in-memory DB (H2 or Testcontainers) for integration tests
- Use factories/builders for test data (not hard-coded IDs)
- Reset DB state between tests (`@Transactional` rollback or `@DirtiesContext`)
- Never use real external services — mock all HTTP clients

## Testcontainers Pattern (Java)

```java
@Testcontainers
class OrderIT {
  @Container
  static PostgreSQLContainer<?> postgres =
      new PostgreSQLContainer<>("postgres:16-alpine")
          .withDatabaseName("test")
          .withUsername("test")
          .withPassword("test");

  @DynamicPropertySource
  static void props(DynamicPropertyRegistry registry) {
    registry.add("spring.datasource.url", postgres::getJdbcUrl);
  }
}
```

## Pytest Fixtures (Python)

```python
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def db():
    # Use SQLite in-memory for speed, or Testcontainers for PostgreSQL parity
    engine = create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:")
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
    yield engine

@pytest.fixture
def session(db):
    with Session(db) as session:
        yield session
        session.rollback()
```

## Frontend Test Pattern

```typescript
// React Testing Library
import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react';
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from '@tanstack/react-query';
import { server } from '../mocks/server';  // MSW mock server
import OrderListPage from './OrderListPage';

beforeAll(() => server.listen());
afterEach(() => server.resetHandlers());
afterAll(() => server.close());

test('shows order list when loaded', async () => {
  render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={new QueryClient()}>
      <OrderListPage />
    </QueryClientProvider>
  );
  expect(screen.getByRole('progressbar')).toBeInTheDocument();
  await waitFor(() => {
    expect(screen.getByText('Order #123')).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
```
